authentication_failure_classifications.observed_authentication_mode: cached Google sign-in / Gemini Code Assist individual tier

approval_mode: local --help verified --approval-mode plan as read-only; actual tool behavior remains unverified until an approved live run
sandbox_mode: local --help verified --sandbox; it is intentionally not enabled because native Windows provider behavior and its effect on proxy routing are unverified
notes: Gemini CLI 0.50.0 was installed from the official @google/gemini-cli package at the user-scoped npm prefix. The local executable and --version output were verified without submitting a prompt.
Local --help accepts --prompt, --output-format json, --approval-mode plan, --skip-trust, and --include-directories. The template keeps plan mode and omits --sandbox and yolo/auto-edit modes.
HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY plus NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS are candidate test variables only; successful interception and CA trust require empirical validation.
The generic runner passes the configured user-scope API key directly to the child environment without serializing its value into the adapter, safety gate, command, logs, or reports.
The runner verifies that the persisted Gemini selector is gemini-api-key before preview and again before live execution.
limitations: The earlier unauthenticated Test A exited 41 before vendor traffic and remains a separate preserved result.

The first API-key-labeled Test A reused cached Google sign-in, reached an unsupported individual Code Assist path, and is preserved as a separate failed-authentication result rather than an API-key-mode result.
The persisted API-key selector and key presence are verified offline, but API-key acceptance can only be established by the separately approved live Test A request.
An empty expected_vendor_hosts list deliberately avoids treating unvalidated host guesses as capture expectations.
